figures.caption: Fig. 28–1.The fields E\FigE and B\FigB and the momentum density g\Figg for a positive electron. For a negative electron, E\FigE and B\FigB are reversed but g\Figg is not.
Fig. 28–2.The volume element 2πr2sinθdθdr2\pi r^2\sin\theta\,d\theta\,dr used for calculating the field momentum.

Fig. 28–3.The self-force on an accelerating electron is not zero because of the retardation. (By dFdF we mean the force on a surface element dada; by d2Fd^2F we mean the force on the surface element daαda_\alpha from the charge on the surface element daβda_\beta.)
Fig. 28–4.The function [math]F(s^2) used in the nonlocal theory of Bopp.
Fig. 28–5.A neutron may exist, at times, as a proton surrounded by a negative [math]\pi-meson.
Fig. 28–6.The Yukawa potential [math]e^{-\mu r}/r, compared with the Coulomb potential [math]1/r.
